3.text: Now Moses<S>4872</S> kept<S>1961</S> <S>7462</S> the flock<S>6629</S> of Jethro<S>3503</S> his father in law<S>2859</S>, the priest<S>3548</S> of Midian<S>4080</S>: and he led<S>5090</S> the flock<S>6629</S> to the backside<S>310</S> of the desert<S>4057</S>, and came<S>935</S> to the mountain<S>2022</S> of God<S>430</S>, even to Horeb<S>2722</S>.

And thou shalt make<S>6213</S> the dishes<S>7086</S> thereof, and spoons<S>3709</S> thereof, and covers<S>7184</S> thereof, and bowls<S>4518</S> thereof, to cover<S>5258</S> withal<S>2004</S>: of pure<S>2889</S> gold<S>2091</S> shalt thou make<S>6213</S> them. <sup>to: or, to pour out withal</sup>

Three<S>7969</S> bowls<S>1375</S> made like unto almonds<S>8246</S>, with a knop<S>3730</S> and a flower<S>6525</S> in one<S>259</S> branch<S>7070</S>; and three<S>7969</S> bowls<S>1375</S> made like almonds<S>8246</S> in the other<S>259</S> branch<S>7070</S>, with a knop<S>3730</S> and a flower<S>6525</S>: so in the six<S>8337</S> branches<S>7070</S> that come<S>3318</S> out of the candlestick<S>4501</S>.

Their knops<S>3730</S> and their branches<S>7070</S> shall be of the same: all it shall be one<S>259</S> beaten work<S>4749</S> of pure<S>2889</S> gold<S>2091</S>.

26.text: Moreover thou shalt make<S>6213</S> the tabernacle<S>4908</S> with ten<S>6235</S> curtains<S>3407</S> of fine twined<S>7806</S> linen<S>8336</S>, and blue<S>8504</S>, and purple<S>713</S>, and scarlet<S>8144</S> <S>8438</S>: with cherubims<S>3742</S> of cunning<S>2803</S> work<S>4639</S> shalt thou make<S>6213</S> them. <sup>of cunning: Heb. the work of a cunning workman, or, embroiderer</sup>

And they shall be coupled<S>8382</S> together beneath<S>4295</S>, and they shall be coupled<S>8382</S> together<S>3162</S> above the head<S>7218</S> of it unto one<S>259</S> ring<S>2885</S>: thus shall it be for them both<S>8147</S>; they shall be for the two<S>8147</S> corners<S>4740</S>. <sup>coupled: Heb. twinned</sup>

And thou shalt make<S>6213</S> bars<S>1280</S> of shittim<S>7848</S> wood<S>6086</S>; five<S>2568</S> for the boards<S>7175</S> of the one<S>259</S> side<S>6763</S> of the tabernacle<S>4908</S>,

And the middle<S>8484</S> bar<S>1280</S> in the midst<S>8432</S> of the boards<S>7175</S> shall reach<S>1272</S> from end<S>7097</S> to end<S>7097</S>.

And thou shalt rear up<S>6965</S> the tabernacle<S>4908</S> according to the fashion<S>4941</S> thereof which was shewed<S>7200</S> thee in the mount<S>2022</S>.

And thou shalt make<S>6213</S> a vail<S>6532</S> of blue<S>8504</S>, and purple<S>713</S>, and scarlet<S>8144</S> <S>8438</S>, and fine twined<S>7806</S> linen<S>8336</S> of cunning<S>2803</S> work<S>4639</S>: with cherubims<S>3742</S> shall it be made<S>6213</S>:

27.text: And thou shalt make<S>6213</S> an altar<S>4196</S> of shittim<S>7848</S> wood<S>6086</S>, five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> long<S>753</S>, and five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> broad<S>7341</S>; the altar<S>4196</S> shall be foursquare<S>7251</S>: and the height<S>6967</S> thereof shall be three<S>7969</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
